# The AI-Augmented Architect

The AI-Augmented Architect is a book and companion website by Klaus Barkhausen.

Planned book release: June 2026.

This site is designed for architects, technology leaders, and program managers who need practical guidance for AI-augmented software delivery, legacy modernization, governance, verification, observability, and enterprise architecture.

## Main pages
- / : site overview and positioning
- /book : book overview and table of contents
- /companion : chapter-to-resource map
- /examples : architecture examples and reference artifacts
- /worksheets : reusable worksheets and forms
- /labs : team exercises and workshop labs
- /author : author profile
- /buy : launch and book inquiry page
- /errata : corrections and clarifications

## Topics covered
- AI-augmented architecture
- delegation and verification
- system boundaries and contracts
- observability and operability
- security, privacy, and compliance
- failure modes and technical debt
- legacy modernization
- build, buy, or modernize decisions
- governance, adoption, and economics

## Companion resources
This site includes practical companion material aligned to the book:
- examples
- worksheets
- forms
- labs
- errata and clarifications

## Author
Klaus Barkhausen
https://klausbarkhausen.com
